title: Forgot anniversary, no idea what to get, changes mind
description: ADHD user who forgot their anniversary is in 4 days. Doesn't know what their partner wants. Gives vague info, changes direction mid-conversation, needs to see options to decide. Classic target user.

turns.message: shit our anniversary is friday and I have nothing. help
turns.intent: Panic opener. No name, no interests, no budget. Just urgency.
turns.expect_ai: Should extract: occasion=anniversary, urgency=high. Should ask about partner.

turns.message: her name is Megan. 6 years together. she's... I don't know, she likes a lot of things? she reads, does pottery sometimes, we cook together on weekends
turns.intent: Gives name + relationship + vague interests. Still no budget. User is unsure.
turns.expect_ai: Should show 2-3 directions. Should ask budget. Should NOT ask more about interests.

turns.message: hmm the pottery one is interesting but actually she hasn't done that in months. maybe the cooking one? idk. under 150 I guess
turns.intent: Changes direction. Gives budget reluctantly. Still uncertain. Wants to see actual products before committing.
turns.expect_ai: Should lock in cooking direction + $150 budget. Should complete and show profile card.

conversation_should: Never ask what she likes after turn 2 (already stated)
recs_should: Items available for quick delivery (anniversary in 4 days)

postRecs.decision: Picks slot 0 or 1 (whichever is most cooking-related). Needs the image + description to feel confident — if it looks like something Megan would use, they'll go for it.
postRecs.cardEdit: Wants to personalize the card — would change generic wording to reference their cooking weekends together.
postRecs.wouldRefine: Only if none of the 3 are cooking-related. Otherwise decides from what's shown.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Clicks buy link, then immediately comes back to confirm purchase and get feedback link.

title: Parent agonizing over teenage son's graduation gift
description: Mom who is overthinking a graduation gift for her 18-year-old son. She knows him well but can't decide because everything feels too generic or too expensive. Gives TOO MUCH info. Contradicts herself.

turns.message: My son Ethan is graduating high school next month. I want something meaningful, not just another gift card. He's going to McGill in September.
turns.intent: Clear occasion and name. Mentions what she DOESN'T want. Going to university = life transition.
turns.expect_ai: Should extract: name=Ethan, relationship=son, occasion=graduation, context=heading to university. Should ask about his interests.

turns.message: He's super into sneakers and streetwear — like obsessively. But I don't want to just buy him shoes, that feels lazy. He also games a lot and just started getting into film photography. Oh and he wants a tattoo but I said not till he's 19 lol
turns.intent: Tons of info. Multiple interests. Explicitly rejects the obvious choice (sneakers). Mentions something new (film photography).
turns.expect_ai: Should show directions weighted toward film photography (new interest = more meaningful) and maybe streetwear-adjacent but not shoes. Should ask budget.

turns.message: the photography direction is cool actually, I didn't think of that. maybe $200-300? he's my only kid so I want it to be special
turns.intent: Picks a direction. Gives budget. Adds emotional weight (only kid).
turns.expect_ai: Should complete with photography direction, $200-300 budget, capture the 'special for only kid' expression.

conversation_should: Lean toward photography (newer interest, more meaningful)
recs_should: At least 1 photography-related item (camera, film, accessories)

postRecs.decision: Reads every detail carefully. Will pick the most photography-specific item. Needs the 'why this fits' to reference Ethan's new interest specifically, not generic 'your son will love this'.
postRecs.cardEdit: Would write her own card message entirely — too personal for AI-generated text.
postRecs.wouldRefine: If no photography item is in the picks, would hit 'Not quite right' and say 'I specifically want something for film photography'.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Researches the product externally before buying. May not confirm purchase in-app.

title: New employee trying to get a holiday gift for entire team
description: Started a new job 3 months ago. Office does a holiday gift exchange but they barely know their assigned person. Has almost no info. Terrified of getting something weird or offensive.

turns.message: I got assigned someone for our office secret santa and I literally know nothing about them except their name is David and he works in accounting
turns.intent: Minimal info. Professional context. Fear of awkwardness. Secret Santa = typically $25-50.
turns.expect_ai: Should extract: name=David, relationship=coworker, occasion=secret santa/holiday. Should ask what they DO know or suggest safe directions.

turns.message: I think he drinks coffee? I've seen him with a mug. He seems quiet, maybe early 30s. Our budget limit is $40. I just don't want to get something embarrassing
turns.intent: Very thin signals. Coffee is a guess. Budget is firm ($40 cap). Primary concern is not looking stupid.
turns.expect_ai: Should show safe, crowd-pleasing directions. Coffee is the strongest signal. Should NOT ask more questions — there's nothing more to learn.

turns.message: yeah coffee is the safest bet, let's go with that
turns.intent: Confirms direction. Wants safety over delight.
turns.expect_ai: Should complete immediately. Coffee direction, $40 cap, professional/safe tone.

conversation_should: Not push for more personal info (they don't have any)
recs_should: Items that work for someone you barely know

postRecs.decision: Picks the first coffee-related item immediately. Doesn't read the other options in detail. Just wants this done.
postRecs.cardEdit: Skips the card entirely — it's a secret santa, no card needed.
postRecs.wouldRefine: Never. Picking the safest option fast is the priority.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Clicks buy, done. Doesn't use feedback link — it's a coworker.

title: Complicated relationship, wants to reconnect through a gift
description: Hasn't talked to their sister in 8 months after a fight. Sister's birthday is coming up and they want to use the gift as an olive branch. Emotionally loaded. The gift IS the message.

turns.message: I need a birthday gift for my sister Priya. We haven't really been talking since last summer. I want to... I don't know, bridge the gap I guess
turns.intent: Emotional opener. The gift is about the relationship, not the object. Vulnerable.
turns.expect_ai: Should handle the emotional weight. Extract: name=Priya, relationship=sister, occasion=birthday, subtext=reconciliation. Should ask about shared memories or what she's into.

turns.message: We used to be super close. She got me into hiking and we'd do these long trail walks and just talk. She also loves tea — she has this whole ritual with loose leaf and fancy cups. I think she started a garden this year but I only know from instagram
turns.intent: Rich context through nostalgia. Hiking = shared activity. Tea = her thing. Garden = new but they're not close enough to know details. Instagram stalking = they miss each other.
turns.expect_ai: Should show directions that reference the shared history. Hiking/tea are the strongest signals. Garden is too uncertain.

turns.message: the hiking one hits different. like it's saying I remember what we had. budget maybe $75-125? I don't want it to seem like I'm trying too hard but also not nothing
turns.intent: Picks direction with emotional reasoning. Budget has emotional subtext — not too much (desperate), not too little (careless).
turns.expect_ai: Should complete. Capture the reconciliation expression. Budget $75-125.

conversation_should: Acknowledge the emotional complexity without being therapist-y
Not be too cheerful or surface-level
recs_should: Items that feel personal, not generic
Nothing that screams 'I googled gift for sister'

postRecs.decision: Reads 'what this gift says' for each option carefully. Picks the one whose message best captures 'I remember us'. The product itself matters less than what it communicates.
postRecs.cardEdit: Would heavily edit the card. Might write something like 'I miss our trail talks. Maybe we can do one again soon.' — deeply personal.
postRecs.wouldRefine: If none of the options feel like they reference shared hiking memories, would refine with 'I want something that reminds her of when we used to hike together'.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Buys carefully, wraps it herself. Sends feedback link with a nervous text message.

title: Everything is wrong and they need a gift in 2 hours
description: Ordered something online, it didn't arrive, event is TODAY. Needs something they can buy instantly — digital gift, local pickup, or experience. Tests the system's ability to handle extreme urgency and pivot from physical to digital/experiential.

turns.message: ok so my friend Jake's birthday dinner is TONIGHT and the thing I ordered didn't come. I need something I can get like right now or send digitally
turns.intent: Extreme urgency. Physical product probably won't work. Needs digital/experiential/last-minute solutions.
turns.expect_ai: Should extract urgency. Should immediately orient toward digital gifts, experiences, or instant-delivery options.

turns.message: he's a huge foodie, loves trying new restaurants. we're in montreal. he's turning 30. maybe $50-80?
turns.intent: Good info dump. Foodie + Montreal + 30th birthday. Budget clear.
turns.expect_ai: Should show food/restaurant experience directions. Should acknowledge the last-minute reality.

turns.message: the restaurant experience one, for sure. something I can print or show him on my phone tonight
turns.intent: Needs something deliverable NOW. Digital or printable.
turns.expect_ai: Should complete with experiential/food direction, last-minute constraint emphasized.

conversation_should: Not suggest items that need shipping
recs_should: Things that can be given same-day

postRecs.decision: Picks whatever is most clearly giftable tonight — needs to be digital, printable, or an experience voucher. Physical products are instant reject regardless of relevance.
postRecs.cardEdit: Skips card — will just say something in person at dinner.
postRecs.wouldRefine: If all 3 are physical products, would immediately refine with 'these all need shipping — I need something I can give TONIGHT'.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Buys instantly, screenshots the confirmation, shows it on phone at dinner.

title: Daughter thanking mom for everything, Mother's Day
description: Close relationship, knows her mom well. Wants something that says thank you for years of support. Budget conscious but willing to spend.

turns.message: Mother's Day is coming up and I want to get my mom Cécile something really nice. She does so much for our family.
turns.intent: Clear occasion, name, emotional context.
turns.expect_ai: Extract name, relationship, occasion. Ask about interests.

turns.message: She's really into wellness — yoga, meditation, skincare. She also loves plants and her little balcony garden. She's 58 and honestly deserves to be spoiled
turns.intent: Rich interest data. Age context. Emotional weight.
turns.expect_ai: Show wellness/garden directions. Ask budget.

turns.message: the wellness self-care one. maybe around 100-150? she never treats herself so I want to do it for her
turns.intent: Picks direction. Budget clear. Emotional reason.
turns.expect_ai: Complete with wellness, $100-150, self-care expression.

postRecs.decision: Carefully considers all 3. Picks the one that feels most like pampering.
postRecs.cardEdit: Edits card to add personal touch about how much mom means.

title: First Valentine's Day, terrified of getting it wrong
description: 6 months together, first Valentine's Day as a couple. Has no idea what the "rules" are. Overthinking everything.

turns.message: ok so valentine's is coming up and this is my first one with my girlfriend Emma. what do I even do?? is it too early for jewelry?
turns.intent: Anxious. Questions about social norms. Name given.
turns.expect_ai: Reassure. Extract relationship context. Ask about her.

turns.message: she's a graphic designer, really creative. loves vintage stuff and thrift stores. she made me a playlist for my birthday which was really sweet. we're both 26
turns.intent: Creative person. Vintage aesthetic. She gives thoughtful gifts.
turns.expect_ai: Show directions. Creative/vintage/thoughtful angles.

turns.message: something creative and personal, not cheesy. maybe 60-100? I want her to know I actually thought about it
turns.intent: Picks direction. Budget. Wants to match her thoughtfulness.
turns.expect_ai: Complete. Creative direction, $60-100.

conversation_should: Not suggest jewelry (he asked if too early)

postRecs.decision: Picks the one that feels most personal and least generic.
postRecs.cardEdit: Writes his own card — wants it to sound like him, not AI.

title: Organizing a group gift for a retiring colleague
description: Collecting money from 8 people for a group retirement gift. Needs something impressive but crowd-pleasing. Committee dynamics.

turns.message: We're doing a group gift for our colleague Tom who's retiring after 30 years. I'm organizing and we collected about 400 from 8 people
turns.intent: Group gift. Big budget. Professional context. 30-year milestone.
turns.expect_ai: Extract group context. Ask about Tom's interests.

turns.message: He's a golfer, loves whiskey, and he and his wife are planning a trip to Portugal for retirement. He's pretty traditional, mid-60s
turns.intent: Clear interests. Traditional taste. Travel plans.
turns.expect_ai: Show directions. Golf/whiskey/travel angles.

turns.message: the travel one is great because everyone knows about the Portugal trip. budget is firm at 400, it's what we collected
turns.intent: Picks direction with social reasoning. Firm budget.
turns.expect_ai: Complete with travel direction, $400 firm.

postRecs.decision: Picks the one easiest to explain to 8 contributors.
postRecs.cardEdit: Card needs to work as from the whole team.
postRecs.wouldRefine: If nothing feels impressive enough for $400.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Buys and reports back to the group.

title: Exhausted new dad getting something for wife's first birthday as a mom
description: Baby is 4 months old. Both parents are sleep-deprived. He wants to make her feel seen and appreciated. Time and energy are extremely limited.

turns.message: My wife Nina's birthday is next week. She just became a mom 4 months ago and she's amazing but exhausted. I want her to feel appreciated, not just get another baby-related thing
turns.intent: Emotional context. Explicit constraint: NOT baby stuff. Wants to honor HER not just her role as mom.
turns.expect_ai: Extract context. Acknowledge the dynamic. Ask about her interests pre-baby.

turns.message: Before the baby she was really into running and barre class, and she loves skincare — had a whole routine. She hasn't had time for any of it. She also misses reading, she used to read a book a week
turns.intent: Pre-baby identity she misses. Running, skincare, reading.
turns.expect_ai: Show directions that honor her identity beyond motherhood.

turns.message: the self-care one. something that says you're still you, not just a mom. 100-200 range
turns.intent: Powerful expression. Budget clear.
turns.expect_ai: Complete with self-care direction, $100-200, identity-affirming expression.

postRecs.decision: Picks whatever most clearly says "this is about YOU not the baby".
postRecs.cardEdit: Writes deeply personal card about how proud he is of her.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Buys immediately — no time to overthink.

title: Sending a care package to a friend going through a tough time
description: Best friend moved to Vancouver 2 years ago, just went through a breakup. Wants to send something that says "I'm here even though I'm far".

turns.message: My best friend Aisha just went through a really rough breakup. She's in Vancouver and I'm in Montreal so I can't be there in person. I want to send her something
turns.intent: Emotional support context. Long distance. No specific occasion.
turns.expect_ai: Extract context. Acknowledge emotional support intent. Ask about her.

turns.message: She loves baking — it's her stress relief. She's also into true crime podcasts and cozy stuff. She's been spending a lot of weekends alone since the breakup
turns.intent: Comfort-oriented interests. Baking as therapy. Loneliness context.
turns.expect_ai: Show comfort/cozy/baking directions. Sensitive to the breakup context.

turns.message: baking stuff or cozy comfort. maybe 50-80? I just want her to feel less alone
turns.intent: Picks direction. Budget. Powerful expression.
turns.expect_ai: Complete with comfort/baking direction, $50-80.

postRecs.decision: Picks the most comforting, cozy option.
postRecs.cardEdit: Writes personal card about missing her and being proud of her.
postRecs.buyBehavior: Ships directly to Aisha with a note.
